TotalEnergies Extends French Fuel Price Caps Through June
headlines.summary: TotalEnergies will keep its fuel price caps across all its services stations in France through June amid the ongoing crisis in the Middle East, the French supermajor said on Wednesday. headlines.title: UK Petrol Prices Hit Highest Level Since 2022
headlines.summary: Petrol prices in the UK have hit a new high since the Iran war began, as the price of oil jumped back up to $100 per barrel following fresh US strikes. The average price of a litre of petrol is now 159.43p in the UK, according to motoring services firm the RAC. This petrol price

headlines.title: Dallas Fed Pres Says World Needs To Consume Less Oil And Gas
headlines.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas President Lorie Logan says that the world may eventually have to reduce its consumption of oil and natural gas to bring volatile energy markets into a balance. headlines.title: Mozambique Contests TotalEnergies' $2 Billion Cost from LNG Project Delay
headlines.summary: The government of Mozambique disagrees with TotalEnergies’ estimate that the years-long delay in the Mozambique LNG project has cost it and its partners $2 billion in overruns, a source familiar with the matter told Bloomberg on Wednesday. headlines.title: China's CNOOC Starts Production at Bohai Sea Oilfield
headlines.summary: China’s top offshore crude oil and natural gas producer, CNOOC, on Wednesday announced the launch of full production from phase one development of the Kenli 10-2 oilfield in the south Bohai Sea. headlines.title: U.K. Energy Bills to Jump 13% as Gas Shock Hits Households
headlines.summary: UK energy prices will jump by 13% from July 1 after energy regulator Ofgem on Wednesday raised the price cap as a result of higher wholesale gas prices amid the Middle East crisis. headlines.title: Oil Prices Fall as Traders Bet on U.S.-Iran Deal
headlines.summary: Oil prices dropped by around 4% early on Wednesday as hopes of a U.S.-Iran deal outweighed concerns about rapidly drawing inventories as the Strait of Hormuz remained closed on the back of fresh U.S.-Iran hostilities. headlines.summary: Emirati ADNOC has sent a second LNG carrier through the Strait of Hormuz, Bloomberg reported today, citing shipping data that shows the vessel is sailing to India.
